pi_items.source: Referenced in task.json description and setup_task.sh
Four test files created in setup_task.sh: hospital_a.hl7, hospital_b.hl7, hospital_c.hl7, hospital_d.hl7
Raw input from setup_task.sh is already formatted correctly. Normalization rule: strip non-digits (5558675309), keep as-is (10 digits, no leading 1), format to (XXX) XXX-XXXX -> (555) 867-5309
Raw input from setup_task.sh is 19850315 (YYYYMMDD). Normalization rule: already in target format.

Raw input from setup_task.sh is 'M'. Setup comment says hospital_a should be 'pass-through'. Normalization rule notes gender should be 'M', 'F', 'O', or 'U'. Input 'M' is already a standard code and should pass through unchanged.
Raw input from setup_task.sh is '555.234.5678'. Strip non-digits: 5552345678 (10 digits, no leading 1). Format to (XXX) XXX-XXXX -> (555) 234-5678
Raw input from setup_task.sh is '03/22/1990' (MM/DD/YYYY). Normalize to YYYYMMDD: 19900322
Raw input from setup_task.sh is 'Female'. Normalization rule: 'Female' maps to 'F'
Raw input from setup_task.sh is '5559876543' (10 digits). Strip non-digits: 5559876543 (10 digits, no leading 1). Format to (XXX) XXX-XXXX -> (555) 987-6543
Raw input from setup_task.sh is '1978-11-05' (YYYY-MM-DD). Normalize to YYYYMMDD: 19781105
Raw input from setup_task.sh is '1'. Normalization rule: '1' maps to 'M'
Raw input from setup_task.sh is '1-555-345-6789'. Strip non-digits: 15553456789 (11 digits). Remove leading 1 (11 digit rule): 5553456789 (10 digits). Format to (XXX) XXX-XXXX -> (555) 345-6789
Raw input from setup_task.sh is '7/4/2001' (M/D/YYYY single digits). Normalize to YYYYMMDD: 20010704 (July 4, 2001)
Raw input from setup_task.sh is 'MALE'. Normalization rule: 'MALE' maps to 'M'

privileged_info_summary: HL7 v2.x healthcare data normalization task using NextGen Connect integration engine. Four synthetic test HL7 messages with patient demographic data from fictitious hospitals (Hospital A-D) with intentionally messy data formats. Test data is generated dynamically in setup_task.sh and includes phone numbers in various formats (formatted, dotted, raw digits, country code), dates of birth in three formats (YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD), and gender values in multiple representations (M, Female, numeric code 1, uppercase MALE). Normalization rules apply standard transformations: phone numbers standardized to (XXX) XXX-XXXX format with leading '1' removal for 11-digit numbers; DOB standardized to YYYYMMDD; gender mapped to M/F/O/U codes. Expected normalized output values verified against transformation rules for all four test cases.
